title: Compute The Haldane Chern Number From Dirac-Mass Signs
summary: Tests whether the topic can derive the Haldane-model Chern number from the two valley masses instead of citing the lattice Chern insulator by name only.
assumptions: The answer must start from the low-energy graphene valleys rather than jumping directly to the final Hall phase.
regime: Topic regression for the Haldane bridge inside the Lecture Two and Lecture Three hall-response family.
scope: Bridge-derivation question for the explicit Haldane lattice criterion.

source_anchors.notes: The answer should write the two valley masses explicitly.
The answer should reconstruct the signed valley contribution formula for the Chern number.

prompt: Derive the Haldane-model Chern number from the valley masses at K and K prime, and explain why opposite mass signs produce the anomalous Hall phase.
pass_conditions: The answer writes the valley masses m_K and m_K prime explicitly.
The answer explains that each massive Dirac cone contributes a half Chern number with opposite valley orientation.
The answer derives C = 1/2 (sgn(m_K prime) - sgn(m_K)).
The answer states clearly when the phase is topological and when it is trivial.
retrieval_hints: Use in regression runs to ensure the local Haldane bridge is derivable rather than merely name-dropped.
